preamble: Fricativization / Lenition. The velar nasal [ŋ] loses its nasality and becomes a voiced velar fricative [ɣ]. This contextual weakening is seen in some dialects where nasal sounds undergo lenition.

examples.note: Contextual weakening of velar nasals in some dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
